summary.intent: Add a 5-second sleep after each Jenkins job trigger for build-fbc (layered operators) to prevent timestamp collisions when multiple FBC builds start simultaneously. Exclude the openshift-group path since it only triggers one FBC job.
summary.outcome: Successfully added sleep after non-openshift fallback start_build_fbc call (~line 245), removed unnecessary sleep from openshift-group path, created branch fbc-build-sleep, committed changes with inline comment explaining collision avoidance, pushed to origin, and opened PR #2695 against openshift-eng/art-tools prod/main.

repo: OCP_TARGET_VERSIONS loop already has a 10-second sleep between iterations
openshift-group path only triggers one FBC job so doesn't need collision prevention
Non-openshift fallback path can trigger multiple FBC jobs simultaneously causing timestamp collisions

code.finding: Sleep added after non-openshift fallback start_build_fbc call to prevent FBC build timestamp collisions

workflow: Pre-commit hooks may require broader permissions than sandbox provides
PR workflow: fork (ashwindasr/art-tools) pushes to origin, PR targets upstream prod/main (openshift-eng/art-tools)

friction: Pre-commit hook failed initially due to sandbox permission restrictions, required retry outside sandbox
